subject: Computer Basics
question_text: Which of the following properties ensures that an algorithm terminates after a finite number of steps?

explanation.concept: Finiteness ensures that an algorithm completes its execution after a limited number of steps, preventing infinite loops.
step_by_step: Finiteness defines limited number of steps
Other properties relate to clarity or feasibility

why_correct.B: Correct because it guarantees termination

why_incorrect.A: Ensures clarity of each step, not termination
why_incorrect.C: Ensures steps are executable, not finite

subject: Computer Basics
question_text: Which characteristic of an algorithm ensures that each instruction is unambiguous and clearly defined?

explanation.concept: Definiteness ensures that every step in the algorithm is precisely stated and leaves no ambiguity.

why_correct.B: Correct because it ensures clear instructions

why_incorrect.C: Relates to applicability to multiple inputs

subject: Computer Basics
question_text: Which of the following best represents an algorithm for finding the maximum of three numbers a, b, and c?

options.text: If a > b then max = a else max = b; If c > max then max = c
Compare a, b, and c simultaneously and assign largest
If a > b and a > c then max = b else max = c

solution.final_answer: If a > b then max = a else max = b; If c > max then max = c

explanation.concept: The algorithm must compare values systematically and update the maximum correctly.
step_by_step: First compare a and b to get temporary max
Then compare c with this max
Thus option A correctly determines maximum

why_correct.A: Correct because it compares all values logically

subject: Computer Basics
question_text: Which term refers to the number of steps required by an algorithm as a function of input size?

explanation.concept: Time complexity measures how the running time of an algorithm grows with input size.
step_by_step: Identify measure related to number of steps
Space complexity refers to memory usage

why_correct.B: Correct as it measures computational steps

subject: Computer Basics
question_text: Which of the following algorithms has the best average-case time complexity for searching in a sorted list?

explanation.concept: Binary search divides the search space in half each step, achieving logarithmic time complexity.
step_by_step: Binary search reduces search space exponentially

why_correct.B: Correct due to logarithmic efficiency

why_incorrect.C: Less efficient than binary in average case
why_incorrect.D: Depends on distribution, not always best

subject: Computer Basics
question_text: Which control structure is primarily used when the number of iterations is unknown beforehand?

explanation.concept: While loops are used when iterations depend on a condition rather than a fixed count.
step_by_step: While loop runs until condition fails

why_correct.B: Correct as it supports unknown iteration counts

subject: Computer Basics
question_text: Which paradigm divides a problem into smaller subproblems, solves them recursively, and combines results?

explanation.concept: Divide and Conquer breaks a problem into smaller parts, solves recursively, then merges solutions.
step_by_step: Thus correct answer is Divide and Conquer

why_correct.C: Correct because it matches divide-solve-combine pattern

subject: Computer Basics
question_text: Which of the following best defines an algorithm?

options.text: A program written in a specific language
A step-by-step procedure to solve a problem

solution.final_answer: A step-by-step procedure to solve a problem

explanation.concept: An algorithm is a well-defined sequence of steps used to solve a specific problem.
step_by_step: It must be structured and purposeful
Other options do not meet definition

why_correct.B: Correct as it matches standard definition

why_incorrect.A: Program is implementation, not algorithm itself

subject: Computer Basics
question_text: Which representation of an algorithm uses graphical symbols like rectangles and diamonds?

explanation.concept: Flowcharts use standard symbols to represent algorithm steps visually.

why_correct.B: Correct because it uses visual symbols

subject: Computer Basics
question_text: Which factor primarily affects the space complexity of an algorithm?

explanation.concept: Space complexity depends on memory usage, which often increases with input size.
step_by_step: Other options relate to time or hardware

why_correct.A: Correct because memory usage scales with input size

why_incorrect.D: Hardware does not define algorithm complexity

subject: Computer Basics
question_text: Which of the following best describes the worst-case time complexity of linear search in an unsorted array of size n?

explanation.concept: In worst case, linear search checks every element, leading to O(n) complexity.
step_by_step: In worst case, element is last or absent
All n elements must be checked

why_correct.C: Correct because every element may be checked once

subject: Computer Basics
question_text: Which type of algorithm builds the solution step-by-step by always choosing the next optimal choice?

explanation.concept: Greedy algorithms make the locally optimal choice at each step with the hope of reaching global optimum.
step_by_step: Other paradigms involve recursion or exhaustive search

why_correct.B: Correct because it selects best option at each step

subject: Computer Basics
question_text: Which scenario indicates that an algorithm is inefficient despite being correct?

options.text: Uses excessive time or memory for large inputs

solution.final_answer: Uses excessive time or memory for large inputs

explanation.concept: Algorithm efficiency depends on time and space complexity; high resource usage indicates inefficiency.
step_by_step: High time or space consumption = inefficient

why_correct.B: Correct because inefficiency relates to resource usage

why_incorrect.C: Multiple solutions do not imply inefficiency
why_incorrect.D: Recursion is a method, not inefficiency indicator

subject: Computer Basics
question_text: Which algorithmic structure ensures that instructions are executed one after another without any branching?

explanation.concept: Sequence structure executes statements in a linear order without conditions or loops.

why_correct.C: Correct because it represents linear execution

subject: Computer Basics
question_text: Which of the following is NOT a valid criterion for evaluating an algorithm?

explanation.concept: Algorithms must be unambiguous; ambiguity is undesirable and not an evaluation criterion.
step_by_step: Ambiguity is a flaw, not criterion

why_correct.C: Correct because ambiguity is not desirable

subject: Computer Basics
question_text: Which concept allows storing results of expensive function calls and reusing them to avoid recomputation?

explanation.concept: Memoization stores computed results to optimize recursive or repetitive computations.

why_correct.B: Correct because it caches results

subject: Computer Basics
question_text: Which algorithmic approach is most suitable when all possible solutions must be explored to find the correct one?

explanation.concept: Backtracking systematically explores all possibilities and abandons invalid paths.

why_correct.B: Correct because it checks all possibilities

subject: Computer Basics
question_text: Which term refers to the maximum amount of memory required during algorithm execution?

explanation.concept: Space complexity measures the maximum memory used by an algorithm.
step_by_step: Thus correct answer is space complexity

why_correct.B: Correct because it measures memory requirement

subject: Computer Basics
question_text: Which situation best demonstrates the trade-off between time complexity and space complexity?

options.text: Using lookup tables to reduce computation time

solution.final_answer: Using lookup tables to reduce computation time

explanation.concept: Time-space trade-off involves using more memory to reduce execution time or vice versa.
step_by_step: Identify scenario involving memory vs time
Thus correct answer is option B

why_correct.B: Correct because it trades memory for speed

subject: Computer Basics
question_text: Which algorithmic property ensures that an algorithm produces the correct output for all valid inputs?

explanation.concept: Correctness ensures that an algorithm yields accurate results for every valid input.

why_correct.B: Correct because it ensures valid results

subject: Computer Basics
question_text: Which of the following scenarios best illustrates the concept of recursion in algorithms?

options.text: Repeating a loop until a condition is met
A function calling itself to solve smaller instances of a problem
Breaking a problem into independent tasks executed sequentially

solution.final_answer: A function calling itself to solve smaller instances of a problem

explanation.concept: Recursion involves a function solving a problem by calling itself with reduced input until a base condition is met.

why_correct.B: Correct because recursion is self-referential problem solving

subject: Computer Basics
question_text: Which component is essential in every recursive algorithm to prevent infinite recursion?

explanation.concept: A base case defines the stopping condition for recursion, ensuring termination.

why_correct.B: Correct because it halts recursion

subject: Computer Basics
question_text: Which condition must be satisfied for an algorithm to be considered deterministic?

options.text: Produces different outputs for same input
Produces the same output for a given input every time

solution.final_answer: Produces the same output for a given input every time

explanation.concept: Deterministic algorithms always produce the same output for a given input.

why_correct.B: Correct because determinism ensures consistency

subject: Computer Basics
question_text: Which of the following best represents an algorithm's scalability?

options.text: Ability to handle only small inputs efficiently
Performance remains acceptable as input size grows
Algorithm uses fixed number of steps

solution.final_answer: Performance remains acceptable as input size grows

explanation.concept: Scalability refers to how well an algorithm performs as input size increases.
step_by_step: It relates to performance with growth

why_correct.B: Correct because it reflects performance with large inputs

why_incorrect.C: Memory usage alone doesn't define scalability

subject: Computer Basics
question_text: Which algorithmic representation is closest to actual programming code but written in plain language?

explanation.concept: Pseudocode represents algorithms in structured plain language similar to programming logic.

why_correct.B: Correct because it resembles code logic

subject: Computer Basics
question_text: Which type of algorithm guarantees finding the optimal solution by considering all possible configurations?

explanation.concept: Exhaustive search evaluates all possible solutions to ensure optimality.
step_by_step: Other methods may miss optimal solution

why_correct.C: Correct because it ensures optimal result

subject: Computer Basics
question_text: Which algorithm design technique is most suitable when subproblems overlap significantly?

explanation.concept: Dynamic programming solves overlapping subproblems efficiently by storing results.
step_by_step: Thus correct answer is dynamic programming

why_correct.B: Correct because it handles overlapping subproblems efficiently

subject: Computer Basics
question_text: Which term describes how well an algorithm utilizes system resources like CPU and memory?

explanation.concept: Efficiency measures how effectively an algorithm uses time and space resources.

why_correct.B: Correct because it reflects resource utilization

subject: Computer Basics
question_text: Which scenario best represents a non-deterministic algorithm?

options.text: Algorithm always follows a fixed sequence of steps
Algorithm produces the same output for same input
Algorithm may take different execution paths for the same input

solution.final_answer: Algorithm may take different execution paths for the same input

explanation.concept: Non-deterministic algorithms may behave differently for the same input due to multiple possible execution paths.
step_by_step: Same input may lead to different executions

why_correct.C: Correct because it defines non-deterministic behavior

subject: Computer Basics
question_text: Which aspect of an algorithm determines how easy it is to understand and modify?

explanation.concept: Readability refers to how easily humans can understand and maintain an algorithm.

why_correct.B: Correct because it impacts understanding and modification
